content: read reviews/615.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/615.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/615.md.

# Review: `deepmind/615.lean` ‚Äî Erd≈ës Problem 615

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 615`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/615](https://www.erdosproblems.com/615)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[EHSSS93]`, `[EHSS83]`, `[Su03]`, `[FLZ15]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos615 ... end Erdos615`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos615`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Classical`.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsIndependentSet` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `ramseyTuranNumber` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `ramseyTuranNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `IsIndependentSet` (a `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] `ramseyTuranNumber` (returning `‚Ñï`) uses `lowerCamelCase`.
- [ ] N/A ‚Äî No `local notation` needed; no problem-specific notation used.
- [ ] N/A ‚Äî No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity-check declarations, but this is optional.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_615` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing source papers: `[EHSSS93]`, `[FLZ15]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks: *"Does there exist some constant c > 0 such that ‚Ä¶"* ‚Äî this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1). The problem has been **disproved** by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15], so the answer is "no".

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(False) ‚Üî P`, correct for a disproved yes/no question.
- [x] `answer(False)` replaces `answer(sorry)` because the problem has been solved (negatively).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked** ‚Äî existence of a constant `c > 0` such that graphs with ‚â• (1/8 ‚àí c)n¬≤ edges must contain a K‚ÇÑ or a large independent set ‚Äî so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no".
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(False)` on the RHS of `‚Üî`, not as theorem parameters. The theorem has no parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is a valid category (`research solved`).
- [x] Main theorem: `research solved` with `sorry` proof ‚Äî acceptable (the "no sorry for open" rule applies only to `research open`).
- [x] Variant theorem also has `@[category research solved]`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute on each theorem: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Main theorem uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] Variant also uses multi-line `by\n  sorry`.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_615.variants.EHSS` ‚Äî follows the `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` pattern.
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring documenting the Erd≈ës-Hajnal-S√≥s-Szemer√©di result [EHSS83].

Note on the variant: `erdos_615.variants.EHSS` takes `(Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0)` as theorem parameters. This is a **direct assertion** (Case 3) with no `answer()` wrapper, so the quantifier-as-parameter rule from the `answer()` common rules does not apply. Parameters are acceptable here.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚àà`, `‚â•`, `‚â§`, `¬¨`, `‚Üî`, `‚àß`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñï`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all <100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (static review only ‚Äî requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**No issues found.** The file fully conforms to the checklist.

- The copyright header, import, module docstring, and namespace are all correct.
- Custom definitions (`IsIndependentSet`, `ramseyTuranNumber`) are well-documented, correctly cased, and appropriately marked `noncomputable`.
- The main theorem correctly uses `answer(False) ‚Üî P` (Case 1) for a disproved yes/no question, with all quantifiers in the statement body rather than as theorem parameters.
- The variant `erdos_615.variants.EHSS` is properly named, documented, attributed, and uses a direct assertion (Case 3) with parameters ‚Äî which is appropriate since it does not use `answer()`.
- Category (`research solved`) and AMS (`5`) attributes are valid and correctly formatted on both theorems.
- Code quality is clean throughout.

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 615
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/615](https://www.erdosproblems.com/615)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDoes there exist some constant $c > 0$ such that if $G$ is a graph with $n$ vertices
    25‚Üíand $\geq (1/8 - c)n^2$ edges then $G$ must contain either a $K_4$ or an independent set
    26‚Üíon at least $n / \log n$ vertices?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíEquivalently, if $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$ denotes the Ramsey-Tur√°n number (maximum number of
    29‚Üíedges in a $K_k$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence number $< \ell$), the
    30‚Üíquestion asks whether $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \lceil n / \log n \rceil) < (1/8 - c)n^2$ for some
    31‚Üí$c > 0$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Hajnal, Simonovits, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSSS93].
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíErd≈ës, Hajnal, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSS83] proved that for any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$,
    36‚Üí$$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \varepsilon n) < (1/8 + o(1))n^2.$$
    37‚Üí
    38‚ÜíSudakov [Su03] proved that $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) = o(n^2)$ whenever
    39‚Üí$f(n)/\sqrt{\log n} \to \infty$.
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíDisproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15], who showed that
    42‚Üí$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) \geq (1/8 - o(1))n^2$
    43‚Üíwhenever $f(n) = o(\sqrt{\log n / \log \log n})$.
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí**References:**
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[EHSSS93] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., Simonovits, M., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1993).
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[EHSS83] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1983).
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí[Su03] Sudakov, B. (2003).
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí[FLZ15] Fox, J., Loh, P.-S., and Zhao, Y. (2015).
    54‚Üí-/
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Classical
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üínamespace Erdos615
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- A set of vertices is independent in $G$ if no two distinct vertices in the set
    61‚Üí    are adjacent. -/
    62‚Üídef IsIndependentSet {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    63‚Üí  ‚àÄ u ‚àà S, ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- The Ramsey-Tur√°n number $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$: the maximum number of edges in a graph
    66‚Üí    on $n$ vertices that contains no $k$-clique and has no independent set of size
    67‚Üí    $\geq \ell$. Returns $0$ if no such graph exists. -/
    68‚Üínoncomputable def ramseyTuranNumber (n k ‚Ñì : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    69‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    70‚Üí    G.edgeFinset.card = m ‚àß G.CliqueFree k ‚àß
    71‚Üí    ‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí S.card < ‚Ñì}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/--
    74‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 615** (DISPROVED) [EHSSS93]:
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíDoes there exist $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$, every graph $G$
    77‚Üíon $n$ vertices with at least $(1/8 - c)n^2$ edges contains either a $K_4$ or an
    78‚Üíindependent set of size at least $n / \log n$?
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíDisproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15].
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_615 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    84‚Üí    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    85‚Üí      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    86‚Üí        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1 / 8 - c) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 ‚Üí
    87‚Üí        (¬¨G.CliqueFree 4) ‚à®
    88‚Üí        ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (n : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚àß
    89‚Üí          IsIndependentSet G S := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/--
    93‚Üí**Erd≈ës-Hajnal-S√≥s-Szemer√©di** [EHSS83]:
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíFor any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$, every $K_4$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence
    96‚Üínumber $< \varepsilon n$ has fewer than $(1/8 + o(1))n^2$ edges.
    97‚Üí-/
    98‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    99‚Üítheorem erdos_615.variants.EHSS (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0) :
   100‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
   101‚Üí      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
   102‚Üí        G.CliqueFree 4 ‚Üí
   103‚Üí        (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí (S.card : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
   104‚Üí        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (1 / 8 + Œ¥) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 := by
   105‚Üí  sorry
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üíend Erdos615
   108‚Üí

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 615

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/615](https://www.erdosproblems.com/615)

Does there exist some constant $c > 0$ such that if $G$ is a graph with $n$ vertices
and $\geq (1/8 - c)n^2$ edges then $G$ must contain either a $K_4$ or an independent set
on at least $n / \log n$ vertices?

Equivalently, if $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$ denotes the Ramsey-Tur√°n number (maximum number of
edges in a $K_k$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence number $< \ell$), the
question asks whether $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \lceil n / \log n \rceil) < (1/8 - c)n^2$ for some
$c > 0$.

A problem of Erd≈ës, Hajnal, Simonovits, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSSS93].

Erd≈ës, Hajnal, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSS83] proved that for any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$,
$$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \varepsilon n) < (1/8 + o(1))n^2.$$

Sudakov [Su03] proved that $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) = o(n^2)$ whenever
$f(n)/\sqrt{\log n} \to \infty$.

Disproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15], who showed that
$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) \geq (1/8 - o(1))n^2$
whenever $f(n) = o(\sqrt{\log n / \log \log n})$.

**References:**

[EHSSS93] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., Simonovits, M., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1993).

[EHSS83] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1983).

[Su03] Sudakov, B. (2003).

[FLZ15] Fox, J., Loh, P.-S., and Zhao, Y. (2015).
-/

open SimpleGraph Classical

namespace Erdos615

/-- A set of vertices is independent in $G$ if no two distinct vertices in the set
    are adjacent. -/
def IsIndependentSet {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ u ‚àà S, ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v

/-- The Ramsey-Tur√°n number $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$: the maximum number of edges in a graph
    on $n$ vertices that contains no $k$-clique and has no independent set of size
    $\geq \ell$. Returns $0$ if no such graph exists. -/
noncomputable def ramseyTuranNumber (n k ‚Ñì : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
  sSup {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    G.edgeFinset.card = m ‚àß G.CliqueFree k ‚àß
    ‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí S.card < ‚Ñì}

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 615** (DISPROVED) [EHSSS93]:

Does there exist $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$, every graph $G$
on $n$ vertices with at least $(1/8 - c)n^2$ edges contains either a $K_4$ or an
independent set of size at least $n / \log n$?

Disproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_615 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1 / 8 - c) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 ‚Üí
        (¬¨G.CliqueFree 4) ‚à®
        ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (n : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚àß
          IsIndependentSet G S := by
  sorry

/--
**Erd≈ës-Hajnal-S√≥s-Szemer√©di** [EHSS83]:

For any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$, every $K_4$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence
number $< \varepsilon n$ has fewer than $(1/8 + o(1))n^2$ edges.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_615.variants.EHSS (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0) :
    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
        G.CliqueFree 4 ‚Üí
        (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí (S.card : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (1 / 8 + Œ¥) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 := by
  sorry

end Erdos615
